reviews.review_text: 0/10, couldnt drive in real life anymore, it wasnt as realistic as the game.

Great simulator, not a good game. There's basically no content within the game itself and it's especially flagrant when playing on your own. It's basically a sandbox where you can choose what car to race where, but it's not actually an engaging experience nor has it any incentives to keep going. I find myself remembering about it every 6-10 months, setting it up, playing for like 20 minutes and getting tired of how repetitive and simple it is.

I play this sim with a mouse and keyboard, yet I still get a ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ of enjoyment from it, both from the vanilla packs (specially the monza 66 one with the lotus 49) and the mods that I've installed overtime. It's literally the gmod of racing sims when it comes to moddability and by that alone It's already worth it, and I know that I don't play this game the way It's supposed played (with a steering wheel duh) but even still I've managed to have a time of 8:41.476 in the Nurburgring Endurance map with the Audi R8 lms gt3, probably my proudest achievement.
The community keeps a 12 years old game and updates it to todays standards. Think about a car or a track, you can drive it on AC

Not Recommended

I really wanted to enjoy Assetto Corsa, but after spending time with it, I just can’t recommend it. I was excited to jump in with mouse and keyboard, but setting the game up is surprisingly frustrating and confusing for new players. Most tutorials are horribly outdated, and Windows 11 blocks a lot of the old fixes, making even basic controls feel annoying and broken.

The biggest disappointment is the performance. On popular custom servers like No Hesi, the game runs like garbage. Even with my RTX 4070, Ryzen 5 7600X, 16GB DDR5 RAM, and a huge 32GB page file, I still can’t hold a stable 90 FPS. The game constantly spills over into the paging file, causing heavy stuttering and lag that completely ruins the experience. For a 2014 Unreal Engine 3 game, this level of poor optimization is honestly embarrassing.

Don’t get me wrong — the game has a great community that’s actually one of the best out there, and the driving physics can feel amazing. But it’s clearly designed to be played with a real racing wheel, and a decent one will easily cost you $300 or more. You can try using a controller, but it’s not the same.

In the end, Assetto Corsa isn’t a bad game — it just feels outdated, poorly optimized for modern systems, and extremely unfriendly to new players. The heavy performance issues and steep setup requirements make it not worth the money or the headache. They really need to fix the optimization and make it more accessible.

This game has the best mod community out of any other game I have seen not to mention the realism when driving. The fact that you can feel the weight of the car and speed through traffic all makes really fun to play, modding was a very difficult process at first but once I got that figured out I had a very fun time playing the game. 9/10

You should not pay $20 for the base game expecting it to run smoothly on keyboard and mouse. It technically works, but the physics are slippery and it will only drive you insane. I made this mistake. If you want to enjoy the vanilla version, consider a wheel and pedal.

However, I recently discovered Content Manager, which is free, and it fixes this problem entirely. By downloading it, you get access to shaders, mod compatibility, and a wide range of customization that allows you to tweak the controls, making keyboard and mouse feel buttery smooth. Content Manager is only a couple MB as well. The shaders are great and modernize the 2014 visuals of the game. As for the mods, if you get them from trusted sources like Race Sim Studio for another couple of bucks or manage your way through sketchy websites, it allows you to drive a wide range of cars not included in the vanilla game. You do not need mods to have a great experience with Content Manager, though they only elevate it. 

IN CONCLUSION, for keyboard and mouse users, the $20 vanilla game is more of a blank slate that can get boring and frustrating really quickly. If you're willing to download Content Manager for free and maybe drop another couple of bucks on other mods, I would very much recommend this game, as it makes it one of the best racing simulators out there that is decently realistic.

been playing assetto corsa for a while now and honestly it's the only racing sim that actually feels like driving. the physics are insane — you can feel every bump, every weight shift, every mistake you make. no hand-holding, no shortcuts.
career mode is basically nonexistent and the AI is pretty mid, but that's not why you play this game. you play it to get on the nordschleife at 2am and just... drive. lap after lap until you stop making the same mistake in sector 2.
the mod support is what keeps it alive in 2026. new cars, tracks, even full graphics overhauls — the community has basically rebuilt the game from the ground up at this point.
if you're into cars for real, not just the aesthetic, this game will consume you.
9/10 — just get it.

The graphics are quite realistic, but the physics are much worse. At a speed of 35 km/h, I can't enter the most basic turn, and when I try to brake, I immediately skid, even though the ABS system is present. Sometimes, the cars get confused in the gears. There is a limited selection of cars, and if you want more, you have to purchase them. Additionally, there are only racing tracks, and if you want to drive around the city, you can download various mods, but the game may crash occasionally.I would like the developers to improve the physics and add regular roads for regular driving, as well as SUVs, hatchbacks, and other vehicles that don't accelerate as quickly as racing cars.There are a couple of tracks that you can ride on without any problems, so I recommend them.

The game has many cars and tracks, and it also supports mods, which makes it even better because players can add new content easily. This keeps the game fresh and interesting even after a long time.

Although the graphics are a bit old compared to new games, they can be improved with mods and still look very good. The game focuses more on realistic driving than story mode, which makes it perfect for people who love real racing experience.

Overall, Assetto Corsa is the game i would recommend to other players, specially, the ones who have sim racing setup. Straight 5 stars, NO CAP.
Games amazing, Best physics for a racing game but obviously the learning curve is STEEP

For me assetto corsa was never a sim. hell, i don't even have a sim rig! i haven't even took part in the first career race to this day! i always played assetto modded, i have so many mods that my ssd is literally choking from over eating. i don't even know what the actual vanilla assetto is supposed to look like. the state the vanilla game is in, it should have silently died at launch but thanks to people like Ilja Jusprov and Peter Boese, this game is still home to an ever growing community. truly a product killed by its masters but raised by a community. 3, yes 3 whole games have been released by kunos after this but none of them have even become a speck of dust what this one has become i know the same will remain true for many more years to come in future.

 i myself have become a livery creator in ac now so if you want a livery done for any car you can contact me. i made so many new friends in this community that i can't even count them in my discord dms. i will never get tired of this game ever. coming home to experience racing a golf cart to a t-rex and lamborghini veneno will be my favourite moments to the day i die.

In its base version its ok but if you take the time to install Content Manager + CSP + Pure (google it)
the game comes to life and is rivaling the big new titles like even LMU and so on, highly recomended to register at lowfuelmotorsport, getting the client and syncing their cars and tracks (mods) to get updated fresh vehicles that really changes the game.

If you mod it its absolutely stunning graphics, quite so decent physics and in general a total joy to play, and yes it works on linux, even got a proper guide and installer for that on: https://github.com/sihawido/assettocorsa-linux-setup

So enjoy! (btw this game is the best Kunos has made, it makes a shame out of all their other titles, especially since the GFX looks fantastic with the mods but it has soo much higher fps then their other games)
Game is good love racing but don't have a wheel but for some reason the graphics don't match the pics even on all max settings 1440p don't know why but good sim game
